name: Ricky Pearsall
headline: Big-play efficiency flashes, breakout-or-bust WR4 in San Francisco
body:
Pearsall showed real growth in his nine 2025 games, posting an outstanding 0.504 EPA per play and 62.3 percent success rate while averaging 14.67 yards per catch on a deep 14.11 air yards per target. He turned a modest 9.6 percent target share into 36 grabs for 528 yards, with 25 receptions of 10-plus yards proving the downfield juice, though zero touchdowns on just four red-zone looks capped his 52.6 points (WR57). The leap over his rookie year (0.184 EPA, 1.47 yards per route) signals an ascending player.

The 2024 first-rounder (Pick 31, Florida) sits in a crowded 49ers passing tree, so his fantasy fate hinges on volume that has not yet arrived (under 10 percent target share both seasons) and on positive touchdown regression. The talent and draft capital point up, and an injury ahead of him would unlock WR2 upside. For now he is a high-ceiling WR4 and dynasty buy, a flex dart with breakout equity rather than a reliable starter.
